evals.prompt: I'm upgrading my Scandit Cordova MatrixScan app to SDK 7+. It still uses the old BarcodeTracking API. Please migrate it to the current MatrixScan Batch (BarcodeBatch) API. Here's my file: MatrixScanApp_v6.js
evals.expected_output: The skill renames every BarcodeTracking* identifier to its BarcodeBatch* equivalent (BarcodeTracking -> BarcodeBatch, BarcodeTrackingSettings -> BarcodeBatchSettings, the didUpdateSession listener stays but is now a BarcodeBatchListener, BarcodeTrackingBasicOverlay -> BarcodeBatchBasicOverlay). It also modernizes the construction: BarcodeTracking.forContext(context, settings) becomes new Scandit.BarcodeBatch(settings) followed by context.setMode(barcodeBatch), and BarcodeTrackingBasicOverlay.withBarcodeTrackingForView(...) becomes new Scandit.BarcodeBatchBasicOverlay(barcodeBatch, style) followed by view.addOverlay(overlay). The didUpdateSession callback body and session.trackedBarcodes access are preserved. A summary of the renames is shown and the cordova prepare rebuild step is mentioned.

assertions.text: new Scandit.BarcodeBatchSettings() is present in the output code block
Scandit.BarcodeTrackingSettings is NOT present in the output code block
new Scandit.BarcodeBatch( is present in the output code block
Scandit.BarcodeTracking.forContext( is NOT present in the output code block
context.setMode( is called to register the barcodeBatch mode after the constructor
new Scandit.BarcodeBatchBasicOverlay( is present in the output code block
Scandit.BarcodeTrackingBasicOverlay.withBarcodeTrackingForView( is NOT present in the output code block
window.view.addOverlay( or view.addOverlay( is called with the basic overlay
The didUpdateSession listener callback is preserved (still reads session.trackedBarcodes)
No BarcodeTracking-named type identifier remains in the output code block (a parameter named barcodeTracking may be renamed to barcodeBatch; references in prose to the old name are acceptable)
A summary of the BarcodeTracking -> BarcodeBatch renames is shown
The summary mentions running cordova prepare (or an equivalent rebuild step) after bumping the plugin versions

evals.prompt: Quick question for my Cordova app: is BarcodeTracking the same thing as BarcodeBatch? I see both names in old StackOverflow answers and I'm confused which one to use today.
evals.expected_output: The skill explains that BarcodeTracking is the legacy name and BarcodeBatch is the current name for the same MatrixScan Batch feature — the API was renamed (BarcodeTracking* -> BarcodeBatch*) and the user should use the BarcodeBatch* classes (BarcodeBatch, BarcodeBatchSettings, BarcodeBatchBasicOverlay, BarcodeBatchAdvancedOverlay, BarcodeBatchListener, BarcodeBatchSession) on current SDKs. It notes the listener callback name didUpdateSession is unchanged, and that the modern constructor pattern is new Scandit.BarcodeBatch(settings) + context.setMode.

assertions.text: The skill states that BarcodeTracking is the legacy name and BarcodeBatch is the current name for the same MatrixScan Batch feature (semantic)
The skill recommends using the BarcodeBatch* classes on current SDKs
The skill names new Scandit.BarcodeBatch(settings) plus context.setMode as the modern construction pattern
The skill notes the didUpdateSession listener callback name is unchanged across the rename (semantic)
